The women in the Indus Valley Civilization seem to have been associated with the idea of fertility. Terracotta figurines of almost nude females, sometimes with children or pregnancy, have been found, which may represent a household cult related to fertility. These figurines suggest a preoccupation with fertility, but there is no emphasis on the generative organs, unlike in Mother Goddess cults. The presence of these figurines implies that women may have played a significant role in the religious or cultural practices of the time, possibly related to fertility and household cults.
